items.headline: City Reviews Children and Youth Services Master Plan Status
items.summary: The City Council received an update on implementation of the Children and Youth Services Master Plan, including progress on programs like the Safe Summer Initiative Grant and Youth Intervention Services. This report covers the city's strategic efforts to serve youth across San José.

items.headline: Mountain View zoning hearing for 333 Franklin Street postponed
items.summary: An administrative zoning hearing scheduled for Wednesday, April 8, 2026, was cancelled and rescheduled for April 22, 2026. The project at 333 Franklin Street will be re-noticed with its new hearing date, so residents should watch for updates if this development affects their neighborhood.

items.headline: Bascom Avenue Highway-Rail Traffic Signal Project Advances
items.summary: The city extended its consultant agreement with DKS Associates to continue work on the Bascom Avenue Highway-Rail Traffic Signal Project. This infrastructure project addresses safety at the intersection of highway and rail traffic on one of San José's major corridors.

items.headline: City Council Updates Active Transportation Plan with New Scoring Criteria
items.summary: Cupertino is revising its Active Transportation Plan (Phase 2), including changes to how projects are evaluated and scored. This plan affects biking, walking, and transit infrastructure across the city.

items.headline: City Council approves construction project with new contract award
items.summary: The council adopted plans and specifications for a construction project and awarded a contract to move it forward. Details on the specific project and location were not provided in the agenda summary.

items.headline: Milpitas updates housing and community development plan
items.summary: The council considered a substantial amendment to the city's 2025-2030 Consolidated Plan, which guides housing and community development spending. This could affect future affordable housing projects and neighborhood improvements in Milpitas.

items.headline: 65-unit senior affordable housing complex approved in San José
items.summary: Trillium Senior Apartments at 678 E St John St will add 65 units of 100% affordable housing for seniors, representing a $8.8 million investment. This addresses San José's critical shortage of affordable senior housing options.

items.headline: City Council Approves Emergency Interim Housing Siting Policy
items.summary: The council passed a new policy on March 26, 2026 governing where temporary housing for unhoused residents can be located. This policy will determine which neighborhoods and sites can be used for emergency shelter facilities.
